headline: Samsung Electronics expects to win more customers for advanced logic chips
summary: Samsung Electronics said on Thursday it expects to ‌win more deals to manufacture ‌logic chips using its advanced 2 nanometer process ​in the near future, adding it has been in talks with big tech customers regarding the foundry contracts.  Samsung, which competes ‌with TSMC ⁠and Intel in the contract chip manufacturing business, said it is ⁠in a preliminary review to build a second fab in Taylor in ​the U.S. ​state of Texas ​in line with ‌discussions with customers regarding the potential orders.  Last year, ​Samsung secured a $16.5 ​billion order from Tesla  to ‌produce logic chips.

content.title: Samsung Electronics expects to win more customers for advanced logic chips
content.summary: Samsung Electronics said on Thursday it expects to ‌win more deals to manufacture ‌logic chips using its advanced 2 nanometer process ​in the near future, adding it has been in talks with big tech customers regarding the foundry contracts.  Samsung, which competes ‌with TSMC ⁠and Intel in the contract chip manufacturing business, said it is ⁠in a preliminary review to build a second fab in Taylor in ​the U.S. ​state of Texas ​in line with ‌discussions with customers regarding the potential orders.  Last year, ​Samsung secured a $16.5 ​billion order from Tesla  to ‌produce logic chips.

headline: From Intel to Nvidia, Tech CEOs Are Embracing Quantum. Why It Matters.
summary: Intel  CEO Lip-Bu Tan became the latest technocrat to embrace quantum computing on Thursday as privately held PsiQuantum announced he would be joining the company’s board.  It’s a big step for someone who has previously cited quantum computing as a strategic priority.  Back in February, Tan described quantum as “another big area coming up is around the corner” in relation to how large amounts of data are processed.

content.title: From Intel to Nvidia, Tech CEOs Are Embracing Quantum. Why It Matters.
content.summary: Intel  CEO Lip-Bu Tan became the latest technocrat to embrace quantum computing on Thursday as privately held PsiQuantum announced he would be joining the company’s board.  It’s a big step for someone who has previously cited quantum computing as a strategic priority.  Back in February, Tan described quantum as “another big area coming up is around the corner” in relation to how large amounts of data are processed.

headline: Intel CEO Joins PsiQuantum Board. It’s Another Big Tech Endorsement for Quantum.
summary: Intel  CEO Lip-Bu Tan became the latest technocrat to embrace quantum computing on Thursday as privately held PsiQuantum announced he would be joining the company’s board.  It’s a big step for someone who has previously cited quantum computing as a strategic priority.  Back in February, Tan described quantum as “another big area coming up is around the corner” in relation to how large amounts of data are processed.

content.title: Intel CEO Joins PsiQuantum Board. It’s Another Big Tech Endorsement for Quantum.
content.summary: Intel  CEO Lip-Bu Tan became the latest technocrat to embrace quantum computing on Thursday as privately held PsiQuantum announced he would be joining the company’s board.  It’s a big step for someone who has previously cited quantum computing as a strategic priority.  Back in February, Tan described quantum as “another big area coming up is around the corner” in relation to how large amounts of data are processed.
